content.text:
Memory stage: NoteReconciler v1.
        Namespace: project:<0>

        Decide how candidate notes should update active note memory.

        Return JSON:
        {
          "operations": [
            {
              "action": "insert | noop | supersede | retract | update",
              "candidate_index": 0,
              "target_note_id": "existing-note-id-or-null",
              "patch": null,
              "links_to_create": [
                {
                  "to_note_id": "existing-note-id",
                  "link_type": "supports | contradicts | refines | related | supersedes | derived_from",
                  "link_weight": 1.0
                }
              ],
              "reason": "short explanation"
            }
          ]
        }

        Actions:
        - insert: candidate is new reusable context.
        - noop: candidate duplicates an existing active note or is lower value.
        - supersede: candidate is a better current version of target_note_id.
        - update: patch target_note_id without inserting a new note.
        - retract: target_note_id should no longer be active because TARGET_SOURCE retracts it.

        Rules:
        - Compare semantic meaning, not wording.
        - Do not keep duplicate notes just because titles differ.
        - Prefer updating or superseding an existing note when the candidate refines the same decision, rationale, plan, or procedure.
        - Insert when the candidate captures a distinct reusable semantic fragment.
        - Use noop for summary spam, weak paraphrases, or information already captured by an existing note.
        - Use links_to_create only for strong semantic relations between notes.
        - target_note_id must be one of Existing active notes below or null.
        - candidate_index is zero-based from Candidate notes below.
        - Return one operation per candidate unless TARGET_SOURCE only retracts or updates an existing note.
        - Return valid JSON only.

        TARGET_SOURCE:
        Document title: 03. Prompt Pack
Document source: agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md

id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 query and filter policy summary=`Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3` retrieval plans should include notes for rationale-heavy or plan-heavy material, claims for preferences/status/factual updates, source retrieval when conflicts or grounding checks matter, and time filters for temporal language such as "now", "used to", "from now on", deadlines, or relative dates. Plans should remain precise, preferring fewer better queries. scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. Prompt Pack / 2. id=hot-path:note:<11> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 role and retrieval focus summary=`Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3` runs after the router and before extraction/reconciliation to create a precise retrieval plan for memory update. It should fetch only memories and evidence useful for deduplication, contradiction detection, temporal updates, and scope alignment. scope=Document `agent_memory_handoff/03_prompts.md`, section `03. responseFormat.description: Reconcile note candidates against active memory notes.
responseFormat.name: memory_note_reconciler

systemPrompts:
You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

You are a concise assistant inside a Gromozeka memory e2e test.
Answer user questions directly.
Do not use tools.
If the question asks for only a short value, return only that value.

id=hot-path:note:<3> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Claim Extractor v3 extracts atomic semantic claims summary=`Claim Extractor v3` is invoked when the router chooses `direct_structured_write` or `mixed`, or by a background consolidator, and extracts precise atomic semantic claims from latest turns. id=hot-path:note:<8> type=DOC_DIGEST status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=ProfileUpdater v3 compact profile projection policy summary=`ProfileUpdater v3` rebuilds a compact always-in-context profile projection from explicit user instructions, stable claims, and selected long-lived context after claim/task updates and sometimes note consolidation. id=hot-path:note:<10> type=DIRECTION status=ACTIVE maturity=FRESH title=Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3 query and filter policy summary=`Write-Time Retrieval Planner v3` retrieval plans should include notes for rationale-heavy or plan-heavy material, claims for preferences/status/factual updates, source retrieval when conflicts or grounding checks matter, and time filters for temporal language such as "now", "used to", "from now on", deadlines, or relative dates.
